articles.title: 'Heartbroken and angry': Ottawa man found dead after homecare agency closes his file
articles.description: John LaCombe's body was found the same day the agency called his sister to say they were closing his file — because he wasn't answering the phone.

articles.title: Canada’s wealth inequality gap is widening, could a tax reform fix it?
articles.description: Canada’s wealth inequality gap is widening, so much so that the concentration at the top makes up for a large chunk at the bottom. articles.title: Canadians beware: Reports are mounting of ICE detaining expats who thought their paperwork was good
articles.description: "Now you have an administration that wants to maximize the number of people who are caught up in the dragnet and deported, and so they will draw up a DUI somebody had 30 years ago. Or weed possession from when somebody was 17."

articles.title: Even with new projects on the way, how much space is still needed at Edmonton Public Schools?

articles.title: Hassan Took a Bike Ride. Now He's One of the Thousands Missing in Gaza
articles.description: In a place denied access to basic forensic technology—and where people disappear into Israeli detention—the fate of thousands remains unknown. One of them is an autistic teenager.

articles.title: What Happens When You Can’t Get a Death Certificate in Gaza
